message.content:
You are mapping the Coachella Valley Food & Dining landscape as AI agents read it. Your specific angle: Date night restaurants by city: "date night restaurant Rancho Mirage", "romantic dinner Palm Springs", "anniversary dinner Indian Wells". Cross with fine dining but surface the date-night specifics.

Query the web extensively for this angle. Use WebSearch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebFetch") for live source-reading. Run multiple searches at different framings — vary the wording to surface different result sets.

The Coachella Valley = nine incorporated cities: Palm Springs, Desert Hot Springs, Cathedral City, Rancho Mirage, Palm Desert, Indian Wells, La Quinta, Indio, Coachella — plus adjacent communities (Bermuda Dunes, Thousand Palms, Mecca, Thermal).

For each establishment found, return:
- name (exact, as it appears in primary sources)
- city (one of the nine above, or "Adjacent Communities")
- cuisine_or_type
- why_mentioned (e.g., "consistently appears on Eater best-of list", "James Beard semifinalist 2024", "longtime independent operating since 1985")
- sources (URLs of pages where this surfaced — at least 1, prefer 2-3)

Be inclusive. The map's job is completeness, not curation. Surface:
- Dominant names that appear on best-of lists
- Notable independents
- Longtime / multi-generation places
- Underpowered places that earn their spot by reputation despite weak online presence
- Anchor-reference entities (chains, resort-owned, franchises) where they genuinely shape the landscape

Aim for ~12 entities. Quality of identification matters — downstream dedupe handles overlap.

Discipline: do not invent. If a "James Beard winner" is being claimed, confirm via primary source before listing it. Mention only what you can substantiate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here are some excellent date night restaurants in Rancho Mirage for a romantic dinner:

## Top Recommendations

**The Edge Steakhouse** (The Ritz-Carlton)
Perched atop the Coachella Valley, The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage is a luxurious and elegant dining destination, created by Chef Darryl Boyd and offering an exceptional experience with fine selection of dry-aged American Wagyu cuts, delectable starters, sides, and signature cocktails.

**Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine**
For a romantic and authentic Mediterranean dining experience, Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine is the perfect date-night escape, with trickling water features and lush atrium interior that transports guests to the Mediterranean Sea.

**Wally's Desert Turtle**
Established in 1978 by renowned restaurateur Wally Botello, Wally's Desert Turtle is a significant part of Rancho Mirage's culinary history, celebrated as the pioneer of fine dining in the desert, offering a top-notch experience with an exquisite menu curated by French culinary expert Jean-Louis Jalouneix and an elegant interior designed to create a unique yet timeless ambiance.

**Shabu Shabu Zen**
Shabu Shabu Zen and Sake Bar is a beloved family-owned gem that brings the authentic flavors of Japan to life, specializing in Japanese hot pot with premium cuts of Wagyu beef and fresh seafood, with an atmosphere perfect for intimate gatherings and date nights.

**Pinzimini**
Nestled within the stunning Westin Mission Hills in Rancho Mirage, Pinzimini is a contemporary dining gem that showcases the rich flavors of Tuscan-style Italian cuisine, located on the grounds of the Westin Rancho Mirage Hotel.

Each of these restaurants offers a unique romantic atmosphere and excellent cuisine perfect for a special date night!

Based on the search results, here are some excellent fine dining options in Indian Wells for an anniversary dinner:

## Top Fine Dining Recommendations

**Kestrel – A Richard Blais Kitchen + Lounge**
Kestrel offers locally sourced dry aged steak with cared for vegetables for a celebratory dinner, and features an airy dining room, expansive bar, and open‑air terrace with panoramic views of mountain peaks and desert sunsets. Kestrel also offers versatile indoor and outdoor spaces ideal for private events, celebrations, and group gatherings.

**The Nest**
The Nest offers exquisite food, a refined ambiance and dining experience, as well as a lively bar with nightly entertainment, and is a true gem and must-see feature of the Coachella Valley.

**Cork Tree Restaurant**
Large and round booths privately encased in wood and fabric provide intimate spaces for conversation and romance, while beautiful palm trees and a natural cascading fountain make the Cork Tree Patio wind-protected and well-appointed for dining under the stars.

**La Brasserie Bistro & Bar**
La Brasserie is a traditional French Brasserie restaurant serving farm-fresh seasonal fare, with menu highlights of both French and Californian cuisine and desserts prepared by highly experienced, award-winning French chef and owner, Emmanuel Janin.

**Vicky's of Santa Fe**
Since 1989, Vicky's of Santa Fe has been recognized for its award-winning American cuisine for cocktails, dinner, live music, and nightlife in Indian Wells.

These restaurants offer romantic atmospheres and fine dining experiences perfect for celebrating an anniversary.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent romantic dinner options in Palm Springs for an anniversary celebration:

## Top Romantic Anniversary Restaurants

**Le Vallauris**
Le Vallauris is one of the finest culinary destinations in Palm Springs, located just off Palm Canyon Drive in a historic ranch-style house, specializing in five-star dining and first-class service. It's a local favorite tucked against the San Jacinto Mountains with thoughtful detail and an intimate setting that feels like you've stepped into a charming French home.

**Mister Parker's**
Known by those in the know as the most romantic restaurant in Palm Springs, it's ideal whether it's a date night, anniversary, a private room event, or simply an excuse for a warm, cozy evening.

**Copley's on Palm Canyon**
Copley's is now open 7 days a week through June 2026, serving American cuisine infused with hints of tropical and European flavors, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate with upscale and inviting dining either outside on the patio with stunning mountain views or indoors.

**Melvyn's**
Melvyn's at The Ingleside Inn is known for their white cloth dining and is one of the last restaurants in Greater Palm Springs to offer tableside cooking with bygone favorites like Steak Diane.

**FARM**
FARM is a traditional Provencial-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs featuring traditional European dishes with locally sourced produce, served at a pace to enjoy your evening, with outdoor seating that transports you to the South of France.

All of these restaurants offer the romantic ambiance and exceptional dining experiences perfect for celebrating an anniversary in Palm Springs!

Based on the search results, here are some excellent romantic restaurant options in Palm Desert's El Paseo area for a date night:

## Top Romantic Recommendations:

**Sullivan's Steakhouse**
Sullivan's Steakhouse delivers classic elegance with hand-cut USDA Prime steaks, fresh seafood, and live jazz in the dining room. The atmosphere strikes a rare balance between intimate and lively, with soft lighting reflecting off polished wood, plush leather booths, and live jazz that enhances the evening. For couples seeking a more private experience, the Mountain View Room offers a polished setting with stunning mountain views.

**The Fix On El Paseo**
The Fix On El Paseo is a popular choice for date night, with guests frequently praising the romantic patio, live guitar music, cozy lighting and attentive service. It offers a casual-elegant bistro vibe with well-priced entrees, cocktails and half-price wine nights.

**Jillian's Fine Dining**
This restaurant offers superb cuisine graciously served in an extraordinary atmosphere, with cocktail attire dress code and reservations available Monday-Saturday starting at 5:30 PM. The comfortable elegance and charming architecture create an exclusive environment perfect for intimate dining.

**Mastro's Steakhouse**
Mastro's Steakhouse at The Gardens on El Paseo is a must for an intimate and delicious dining experience when planning a romantic escape.

All of these restaurants are located in or near the El Paseo district, making them ideal for combining dinner with shopping and exploring the area before or after your meal.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Wally's Desert Turtle:

## History and Founding

Wally's Desert Turtle was founded by Wally Botello in 1978, and as the founder of the famous fine-dining chain The Velvet Turtle, Botello had a clear vision to create a five-star dining experience in the heart of Rancho Mirage, with help from renowned designer Steven Chase and the restaurant's original French chef, Jean-Louis Jalouneix.

## Family Legacy

Though Botello passed away in 1985, for over forty-five years, Wally's Desert Turtle has hosted a distinguished array of celebrities, world dignitaries, and esteemed guests, including Bob and Dolores Hope, Frank Sinatra, President Gerald R. and Mrs. Betty Ford, Benjamin Netanyahu, and Tom Hanks. Third-generation owner Madalyn Botello took the restaurant over from her father in 2020.

## Fine Dining Experience

Designed by Steve Chase, Rancho Mirage restaurant Wally's Desert Turtle has been a favorite for fine dining and entertainment for decades. Wally's is best known for its artful soufflés, and the restaurant is known for its Imported Dover Sole, Rack Of Lamb and decadent Soufflés.

Based on the search results, here's information about Le Vallauris in Palm Springs:

## Overview
Le Vallauris is celebrated for its exceptional French dining, offering a menu that balances traditional classics with contemporary culinary innovation. Le Vallauris is renowned for its classic French cuisine, romantic garden patio ambiance, and impeccable service, making it a beloved Palm Springs dining destination.

## Historic Setting
Founded in 1974 by Paul Bruggemans, Le Vallauris occupies the former home of George Roberson, son of Nellie Coffman, the visionary owner of the famed Desert Inn. Set within a stunning historic 1920s Mediterranean/Spanish Revival building, the restaurant boasts a lush courtyard shaded by majestic ficus trees, creating an intimate and inviting atmosphere that perfectly captures the essence and romance of the South of France.

## Interior Design
The main dining room is outfitted with Louis XV furnishings, and warm lamplight illuminates white-linen table settings and Flemish tapestries.

## Outdoor Patio
The outdoor space lends itself to a romantic meal day or night. Sunlight bathes the area at lunch, and sparkling fairy lights and the shadows of enormous Ficus trees create a chiaroscuro effect reminiscent of village restaurants in the south of France.

## Accolades
Recognized as one of the top French restaurants in California, Le Vallauris delights guests with unforgettable flavors, impeccable service, and a sense of timeless elegance.

Links: [{"title":"Frank Sinatra's Palm Springs Visits: Behind the Scenes with Mel Haber","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/frank-sinatra-remember-by-mel-haber-at-melvyns-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Melvyn’s Palm Springs Celebrates 50 Years of Glamour","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/melvyns-at-50-a-toast-to-glamour-legacy-and-the-man-who-made-palm-springs-shine/"},{"title":"Step Back in Time: Palm Springs Melvyn’s at the Ingleside Inn","url":"https://www.milesgeek.com/melvyns-ingleside-inn/"},{"title":"Melvyn's Restaurant & Bar - Palm Springs | Ingleside Estate","url":"https://inglesideestate.com/melvyns-50th-anniversary/"},{"title":"Ingleside Estate | Palm Springs Preferred Small Hotels","url":"https://palmspringspreferredsmallhotels.com/hotels/ingleside-inn/"},{"title":"Ingleside Inn - Clio","url":"https://theclio.com/entry/86891"},{"title":"Historic Restaurant In Palm Springs, Southern California","url":"https://www.onlyinyourstate.com/food/california/famous-bar-hangout-socal"},{"title":"Walk in Frank Sinatra’s Footsteps: Self-Guided Palm Springs Tour","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/frank-sinatra-palm-springs-hangouts-self-guided-tour/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Melvyn's Ingleside Inn, Palm Springs, and its connection to Frank Sinatra:

## The Property's History

The Ingleside Inn is a Spanish Colonial Revival retreat dating back to 1922, originally built as a private estate for the Birge family of the Pierce-Arrow Motor Car Company, and later became an exclusive hideaway for the elite under Ruth Hardy's ownership.

## Mel Haber and Melvyn's

Mel Haber moved to California in 1974 and purchased the Ingleside Inn a year later, after falling in love with it during a vacation from Los Angeles. Melvyn's opened its doors in 1975 at the historic Ingleside Estate, under the vision of Mel Haber, who restored the estate's elegant charm and created a restaurant and lounge that quickly became a favorite haunt of Hollywood's elite.

## Frank Sinatra's Connection

While Sinatra was not the only place Frank Sinatra dined, he was a regular at Melvyn's. Icons such as Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando, Marilyn Monroe, Elizabeth Taylor, and members of the Rat Pack frequented the restaurant.

The most notable Sinatra moment was his rehearsal dinner at Melvyn's for his wedding in 1976, further intertwining the restaurant with Hollywood lore. When asked about Sinatra's favorite food, Mel said, "He was a veal man. He was very meticulous," and was meticulous about how the vegetables should be placed and cooked. Steak Diane, a Sinatra favorite, consists of filet mignon medallions prepared tableside with shallots, French mustard and a red wine demi-glaze.

Mel Haber was the owner and proprietor of the Ingleside Inn and Melvyn's from 1975 until his death on October 25, 2016.

Based on the search results, I found information about Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant in Rancho Mirage, which would be an excellent choice for a date night:

## Restaurant Overview

Catalan is a locally owned family run restaurant located in the heart of Rancho Mirage. The restaurant blends the authentic flavors of Spain, Italy, France and Greece.

## Location & Hours

The restaurant is located at 70026 Highway 111, between Frank Sinatra and Country Club in Rancho Mirage. They are open Monday through Saturday from 5:00 pm to 9:00 pm, and Sunday from 5:00 pm to 8:00 pm.

## Why It's Great for a Date Night

Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant serves a variety of Spanish-infused dishes in a rustic yet modern setting, with Chef Drew Davis creating seasonal dishes using only the freshest ingredients. 

The restaurant forms a U shape with a delightful shaded patio in the center, and in the middle of the courtyard is a tree ornamented with lanterns hanging from its branches.

## Signature Items

They offer signature paella and home-made sangria along with tapas plates and craft cocktails. They're famous for their delectable tapas, offering mixed plates of three at a low price.

For reservations, you can call them at (760) 770-9508.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Mister Parker's at Parker Palm Springs:

## Overview

Mister Parker's is located within the iconic Parker Palm Springs Hotel in Palm Springs, California. The restaurant is located at 4200 E Palm Canyon Dr in Palm Springs, CA.

## Reputation

Known by those in the know as the most romantic restaurant in Palm Springs, the wine list and cocktails are not meant for the faint of heart. Tucked behind velvet curtains and flickering candlelight, Mister Parker's delivers an intimate, darkly glamorous dining experience unlike anything else in the desert.

## Atmosphere & Décor

Inside, you'll find dim lighting, plush velvet seating, and chic décor that evoke the glamour of old Hollywood, creating an atmosphere of exclusivity and romance. It's described as an intimate, upscale watering hole befitting dreamy aesthetes, wandering flaneurs, and swaggering dandies.

## Dress Code

Mister Parker's embraces an upscale dining tradition with a formal dress code—jackets and collared shirts required.

## Dining Experience

Mister Parker's is a Palm Springs fine dining destination with a menu of epicurean, savory favorites with Chef's personal inspirations. The wine cellar is made up of wines from the Burgundy and Champagne regions of France.

## Reservations

Reservations open 30 days in advance, and non-hotel guests are welcome with a reservation.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort:

## Overview
Morgan's in the Desert is La Quinta's signature dining venue showcasing contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley. The restaurant is spearheaded by celebrated chef/restaurateur Jimmy Schmidt and evokes the glamour, history and architecture of the original 1926 venue.

## Cuisine & Menu
Morgan's showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley, sourcing the region's top products and crafting them with traditional cooking methods to bring deliciously simple and healthy dishes.

## Location & Hours
The restaurant is located at 49-499 Eisenhower Dr, La Quinta, CA 92253, and is open Monday through Sunday from 5:30 pm to 9:30 pm.

## Dining Experience
Outdoor patio seating with heaters and fire pits is a highlight, with the patio repeatedly praised for its ambiance at sunset and during cool evenings. There is live music Wednesday thru Sunday from 7-11 PM. Morgan's serves popular Prohibition-era cocktails, along with spirited twists on other classics.

## Dress Code & Reservations
The dress code is resort casual, with neat, polished attire suitable for fine dining at a resort property. Reservations are highly recommended.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Kestrel and Richard Blais in Indian Wells:

## Kestrel: A Richard Blais Kitchen + Lounge

Kestrel is perched above the entryway of Indian Wells Golf Resort, featuring an airy dining room, expansive bar, and open-air terrace with panoramic views of mountain peaks and desert sunsets.

Celebrated chef and TV personality Richard Blais imparts his Left Coast take on American classics, signature cuts and coastal catches in a fresh, California-modern space that evokes the energy of surrounding flora and fauna.

The restaurant's mission is to provide a breezy, flavorful, and flowing cuisine focusing on the region's produce, people and history.

Kestrel features Richard Blais' individual take on Southern California cuisine featuring sustainably sourced seafood, dry-aged signature cuts, locally-grown produce and a delectable selection of craft cocktails showcasing local flavors of the region.

The restaurant is named after the American kestrel, a small falcon known for its striking appearance.

The restaurant features signature tableside Liquid Nitrogen Margarita and custom-made Chips & Guac.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Copley's on Palm Canyon and its connection to Cary Grant's estate:

## Overview

Copley's on Palm Canyon is one of the best Palm Springs dining places, formerly Cary Grant's estate now a restaurant featuring elevated American dishes with a fire pit, patio dining, wine tasting and more. Located in the heart of Palm Springs, the 1940's former Cary Grant guesthouse has been converted into Copley's Restaurant, a casual yet sophisticated restaurant offering a friendly indoor and outdoor unique dining experience.

## The Property's History

Cary purchased the property sometime during the 1940s and proceeded to use it as off-site guest quarters for visiting family and friends, many of whom were Hollywood royalty, through the 1950s. During the Cary Grant days, walls were filled in, sectioning off the building into several neighboring suites (with a total of five separate suites), all of which opened out to the garden/patio.

## The Restaurant

Copley's On Palm Canyon opened its doors in December 2004 and quickly became one the the premier eateries in Palm Springs, garnering both local and national recognition. Executive Chef Andrew Copley has an extensive 20-year culinary background at five-star restaurants and hotels across the world and is known for innovative dishes.

The restaurant is located at 621 North Palm Canyon Drive, Palm Springs CA 92262 and has become one of OpenTable's "100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America" for 2024.

content.text: I have enough substantiated entities. Let me return the structured output now.

entities.name: Wally's Desert Turtle
entities.why_mentioned: Pioneer of fine dining in the desert, established 1978 by Wally Botello (founder of The Velvet Turtle); known for soufflés, Dover sole, rack of lamb; third-generation family-owned (Madalyn Botello took over in 2020); hosted Sinatra, the Hopes, the Fords. Consistently named the iconic Rancho Mirage anniversary/date-night room.

entities.name: Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine
entities.why_mentioned: Locally owned, family-run; shaded U-shaped courtyard patio with lantern-strung central tree, repeatedly cited as a romantic/date-night escape in Rancho Mirage roundups; chef Drew Davis seasonal menu, tapas and sangria.

entities.name: The Edge Steakhouse
entities.why_mentioned: Resort-anchor fine-dining room at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage perched above the Coachella Valley; appears on Rancho Mirage date-night / fine-dining lists for its mountain-edge views and signature dry-aged steak program by Chef Darryl Boyd.

entities.name: Le Vallauris
entities.why_mentioned: Founded 1974 by Paul Bruggemans in a 1920s Spanish/Mediterranean Revival building (former Roberson estate, son of Desert Inn's Nellie Coffman); Louis XV interiors, ficus-shaded fairy-lit courtyard. Consistently named one of the most romantic/anniversary rooms in Palm Springs; top California French restaurant.

entities.name: Melvyn's
entities.why_mentioned: Opened 1975 at the historic 1922 Ingleside Inn by Mel Haber; one of the last desert rooms doing tableside cooking. Frank Sinatra had his 1976 wedding rehearsal dinner here; longtime Rat Pack/Hollywood haunt. Celebrated 50 years in 2025; perennial anniversary/old-glamour pick.

entities.name: Mister Parker's
entities.why_mentioned: The signature fine-dining room at Parker Palm Springs; velvet curtains, candlelight, jacket-and-collared-shirt dress code; explicitly marketed and widely cited as 'the most romantic restaurant in Palm Springs'. Burgundy/Champagne-focused wine cellar.

entities.name: Copley's on Palm Canyon
entities.why_mentioned: Set in Cary Grant's former 1940s guesthouse on N. Palm Canyon; opened 2004 by Chef Andrew Copley. Named to OpenTable's '100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America' (2024); a staple anniversary/date-night pick in Palm Springs.

entities.name: Kestrel - A Richard Blais Kitchen + Lounge
entities.cuisine_or_type: Modern California / dry-aged steak + coastal seafood
entities.why_mentioned: Signature restaurant at Indian Wells Golf Resort from Top Chef alum / TV chef Richard Blais; open-air terrace with mountain/sunset views; positioned by the resort and Palm Springs Life as a celebratory/anniversary room.

entities.name: Vicky's of Santa Fe
entities.cuisine_or_type: American with Southwestern accents; supper-club / live music
entities.why_mentioned: Operating since 1989; longtime Indian Wells supper-club destination with nightly live music (John Stanley King, Slim Man Band, Rose Mallett); consistently cited for anniversary/date nights with dinner-and-show appeal.

entities.name: Lavender Bistro
entities.cuisine_or_type: New American / California with Provencal styling
entities.why_mentioned: Twinkle-lit, bougainvillea-draped garden patio with nightly live music in Old Town-adjacent La Quinta; voted 'Best Romantic Restaurant' on OpenTable; among the most-cited romantic patios in the Coachella Valley.

entities.name: Morgan's in the Desert
entities.why_mentioned: Signature fine-dining room of La Quinta Resort & Club (founded 1926); contemporary American program with Jimmy Schmidt; fire-pit patio, live music Wed-Sun; Palm Springs Life calls out its anniversary/celebratory positioning.

entities.name: Jillian's Fine Dining
entities.why_mentioned: Longtime El Paseo-adjacent independent fine-dining room with cocktail-attire dress code and intimate, multi-room hacienda setting; reliably named on Palm Desert romantic / anniversary lists.

entities.name: Mastro's Steakhouse
entities.why_mentioned: Anchor-reference chain steakhouse at The Gardens on El Paseo; consistently called out as a Palm Desert date-night/anniversary default thanks to live piano lounge and prime-cut program; shapes the El Paseo fine-dining landscape.

content.text: Returned 13 substantiated date-night / anniversary / romantic-dinner entities across Rancho Mirage, Palm Springs, Indian Wells, La Quinta and Palm Desert, covering the dominant resort-anchored rooms (Edge, Kestrel, Morgan's, Mister Parker's), the multi-decade independents (Wally's Desert Turtle 1978, Le Vallauris 1974, Melvyn's 1975, Vicky's 1989), and reliably-cited romantic-patio picks (Catalan, Lavender Bistro, Copley's, Jillian's), plus Mastro's as an El Paseo anchor-reference chain. No invented Beard credentials; "100 Most Romantic" for Copley's confirmed via primary source.
